item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Foundation Operatives have been installed in all levels of the American government with the express purpose of identifying and locating previously uncontained SCP-3155 instances.

I don't think "previously uncontained" makes sense here. Either "uncontained" or "previously contained" would make more sense.

I'm downvoting for clunky wording. It sounds like a failed attempt to sound clinical, unfortunately. The phrasing that sealed my downvote was:

Due to SCP-3155-11's action in injuring SCP-3155-58

I'd be happy to provide additional examples and suggest changes, if that interests you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: surprised nobody has made rdr2 references here

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why must you put two spaces in between words D:

-1 for this incredibly annoying choice of writing; +1 for the article means +0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The woman in the black suit gave me an offer. She told me that Pinkerton could help me control my <Pause> powers, so to speak. My mom, she hadn't ever taught me to control my powers. She said it was a curse, and that it'd be better if I just died. Stopped the blood line. A curse brought from the East by flesh worshipers.

Shouldn't this article be given the Sarkic tag? Or we could even link the Sarkicism Hub to the flesh worshipers.

yayy i did a good

Also check out these wonderful translations by the wonderful translation community!

Japanese translation — by C-Dives
Chinese translation — by varitas096

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: author made edits

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

Firstly, this skip mentions the American Secure Containment Initiative. I propose crosslinking to SCP-2750, which embodies the ASCI pretty well.

Secondly, the anomalous abilities of some of these folk seem to correspond to some SCPs. I propose linking to those.

Editing this:

During this time the American Secure Containment Initiative had disbanded due to lack of government funding during the Civil War.

To this:

During this time the American Secure Containment Initiative had disbanded due to lack of government funding during the Civil War.

Editing this:

On 6-23-1900, SCP-3155-1 was reported to have burst into flames. Intention behind this act is currently unknown.

To this:

On 6-23-1900, SCP-3155-1 was reported to have burst into flames. Intention behind this act is currently unknown.

Editing this:

SCP-3155 instances often have anomalous abilities primarily focusing on combat, although there have been documented cases of SCP-3155 instances controlling the Hume level within an area, effectively acting as a primitive Scrantron Reality Anchor prior to its creation by Prometheus Labs in the early 19██s. Other documented cases have shown flesh manipulation, psychokinetic control, and extreme regenerative abilities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an amazing SCP, mainly because it showcases the fact that some anomalies prefer containment near the end. Combine that with indefinitely colorful character exploration and some history and you've got a real good one. +1, my good sir!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Late response, but it doesn't take place in the modern day in any capacity. Also, I used the footnote because I couldn't find a way to naturally insert it into the first or two sentences.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As usual I'm a sucker for historical skips, and this one came with some nice character exploration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be fair, we don't know when this report was written. The piece could be set in the early 1900s.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The amount of them currently in custody really shouldn't be relegated to a footnote. Also if these individuals are still alive, clearly there is something anomalous about their aging, and there's no excuse for not mentioning it in the description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really good. Couple of typos and formatting errors to be fixed up here and there. (One of the SCP-3155-XX's in the interviews need bolding, and you're missing a space in SCP-3155instances. This is normally the kind of thing I'd have the audacity to edit myself, but doing it on a phone is a pain in the arse, and I'm already late for work as is.)

Great world-building. Immersive dialogue - although you could have perhaps varied the tones and idiolects a little more. Very nice structure. Fun images. Just an enjoyable read all-round. Well done.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is a product of my never ending interest with the Pinkerton Agency as an entity.

Thanks to DrChandra, Modern_Erasmus, and Kakroom for critique.

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:We_never_sleep.jpg for pinkerton logo
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Gruppbild_sv_arbetsf%C3%B6rm%C3%A4n_vid_L%C3%A4ten_Hospitalbygge_-_Nordiska_Museet_-_NMA.0056803.jpg for agents picture
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Baker,_8_Nov_1908_(16555653446).jpg for man
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ballard,_1909_(16394594600).jpg for woman
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1922WomanInHat.jpg for woman2
